raw_consumer_statement:
We rented a car from XXXX ( purchased via XXXX ) for 10 days in Hawaii ( XXXX ). From XX/XX/XXXX to XX/XX/XXXX. A couple days after the return of the car we received a notification that a {$450.00} fee for cleaning will be imposed. See attached pictures taken by the company showing " excessive amount of sand ''. 
First of all, we rented a XXXX not a XXXX, second, the sand was on a floor mat that takes seconds to take out and clean ( a typical amount of sand as shown on the pictures ), third {$450.00}!!!!!!! No mud on the seats, clean body of the car, nothing wet, no spills etc and {$450.00}!!! to clean that? We have a video of the car 's exterior at the drop off, I did not bother to record inside as this never crossed my mind. 
We complained to XXXX and in the end got the charge reduced to {$100.00}. 
In the meanwhile we disputed the charge with Citi, and after some time received a " Resolution '' that I do not think the Citi representative read. In a nutshell it stated we received a partial refund so they are closing it.

We disagree with the entire charge, the fact that it was greatly reduced does not make us ok with it. We would like XXXX to reverse the remaining {$100.00} as well. 
We can take this to court if needed ( for principle, though we understand we would be forced to arbitration before we could even file a small claims court case ), and are also planning to file a complaint with XXXX  and XXXX, but thought we would try here as well.
